content: Okay so I totally forgot about this until just now, so I'm going through it now and I'm hella confused. I have no idea how this is sorted or how it should be split, so I'm just not gonna split it.
though, I didn't get explicit permission from Ace to reupload that file anywhere, so I think I'm gonna wait to add it to my repo until it goes live.
is the repo accessible to all people? if it is then yeah let's wait a bit until the anni version goes live

when we collect contributions to upload them into the game, we need at least: 1) a key column, 2) chinese column, and 3) a translation column.
so as long as you keep those in your file, it should be fine.

you can also download the file to edit locally.

It's accessible to as many people as BIAVGit is, and I did realize that recently, so I'm going to go into my profile and hide it <:karen_nervous:1408324291237511259> 

Cus anyone who looks up "Omnichromia" on GitHub is currently able to see it on my profile, but the same is true about looking up "BIAVGit"

Alright <@1376763322568937562>, I looked it up and while I can hide my personal profile activity, I can't hide my public repos, as any GitHub repo whose visibility is set to "Public" is going to be on the open internet for anyone to find. Currently, in an incognito tab on a completely separate device, I'm able to find both of our repos by searching "Morimens" on GitHub, and when Googling "Morimens Github" the 4th result is Nero's profile, with a direct link to the BIAVGit repo.

The only way to change this is to set the repositories to "Private", which would—if I understand this correctly—result in any account not explicitly given access to the repo by being added as a collaborator seeing a 404 error when following a link to the repo. However, since BIAVGit was established as a Public repo, any forks of it are also public, so if you wanted to make BIAVGit Private, all 19 of its forks and all of their contents would still be publicly accessible to the open internet (and I believe the most active fork would supercede the original repo and links would redirect to it).

What I can do if you're concerned about privacy and security is, since I currently only have 1 fork, it's very likely I can successfully ask <@1400733999000977510> to delete that fork, and I can set MY repo to Private; most if not all of the folks actively working in this server have already been invited to be collaborators for my repo, and I can easily keep up with anyone who needs access after going private.

Thoughts?
I think that's a good idea
I can delete my fork if needed
Please do! You should be a collaborator now so forking & pulling is unneeded.

Sorry if I didn't make it clear!

You don't have to make your entire git private;
this most recent batch of traphase voicelines are unreleased content so we hope to keep them in the locked google drive folder only for the time being

Okay! I'm glad it's not too much of a concern

I still feel like if this project is going to be trusted with unreleased content in any way, that I should do my due diligence and keep any work I'm involved in as secure as I can reasonably make it, so I'm going to set mine to private tomorrow and see if that causes problems.

If it works out well, then I think that would be greatly beneficial in the long run!
